taxonomy: Roegneria hackeliana is a species of bamboo. It belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. Within the family Poaceae, Roegneria hackeliana is classified under the subfamily Bambusoideae, which comprises the bamboo genus. This species is further classified under the tribe Arundinarieae, a group of temperate woody bamboos. Roegneria hackeliana is also known by its synonym, Fargesia rufa.

intro: Roegneria hackeliana, also known as Tibetan timothy grass, is a type of grass plant belonging to the Poaceae family. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Roegneria hackeliana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
